title: Health and safety managers and officers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 58 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Develop, prepare, and coordinate grant applications and grant-related activities to obtain funding for health education programs.
Maintain inventories of hazardous materials or hazardous wastes, using waste tracking systems to ensure proper handling.
Collect and analyze samples of hazardous materials, dust, gases, vapors, or other potentially toxic materials for assessment.
Conduct audits and inspections at hazardous waste sites or industrial sites to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations.
Inspect areas for fire prevention equipment, safety equipment, or first-aid supplies to maintain safety standards.
Develop or maintain medical monitoring and hygiene programs for employees to ensure a safe workplace environment.
Investigate health-related complaints, accidents, or incidents to ensure compliance with public health legislation and improve workplace safety.
Provide safety training and develop materials for staff, including new-employee health and safety orientations.
Collaborate with engineers or health specialists to control or rectify hazardous conditions and improve health outcomes.
Prepare and distribute health education materials and provide public information regarding health issues, programs, and regulations.
Inspect and evaluate workplace environments, equipment, and practices, and recommend corrective measures to enhance safety.
Maintain logs, records, and documentation of activities, inspections, and health education program outcomes.
Prepare and implement emergency response plans and conduct drills to ensure preparedness for health and safety incidents.
Verify availability and monitor the use of safety equipment, ensuring compliance with health and safety standards.
Advise organizations on health education needs, program development, and ensure compliance with environmental and public health regulations.
Develop health education programs and conduct assessments to determine community health needs and service availability.
Obtain and analyze data regarding potential hazards from new equipment or products, focusing on their impact on worker safety.
Inspect camp sites to ensure campers comply with forest use regulations and inspect forest tracts for fire hazards, recommending appropriate fire prevention measures.
Relay messages about emergencies, accidents, crew locations, and fire hazard conditions, while providing initial response to abnormal events or alarms from radiation monitoring equipment.
Analyze samples, such as air or water, for contaminants and monitor personnel to determine radiation exposure levels, notifying supervisors of exposures approaching limits.
Testify in court regarding investigative methods, while preparing reports on contamination tests, decontamination processes, and procedure compliance.
Determine or recommend radioactive decontamination procedures based on equipment size and contamination degree, and implement safety procedures for personnel.
Monitor equipment performance under operational policies ensuring environmental safety and troubleshooting equipment problems as necessary.
Analyze data from processes for efficiency improvements, conducting studies on time, motion, and operational methods in various contexts.
Collaborate with teams to prepare technical documentation and validate laboratory or processing equipment, including writing process specifications.
Develop and implement safety programs addressing materials, processes, and compliance with environmental regulations.
Conduct environmental quality assessments, perform audits, and monitor compliance with relevant regulations including hazardous materials handling.
Coordinate with public services for environmental impact assessments related to construction or traffic safety measures.
Obtain and analyze data regarding industrial processes, recommending improvements for production, quality assurance, and environmental compliance.
Monitor testing procedures to ensure that all tests meet established specifications, and consult with scientists to determine project needs.
Participate in investigations and recommend corrective actions for out-of-specification and failure events.
Receive, inspect raw materials, and ensure regulatory compliance in data from contract laboratories.
Conduct analyses of materials and develop or qualify new testing methods to ensure quality control.
Ensure that laboratory safety and cleanliness standards are adhered to and prepare necessary documentation for testing procedures.
Inspect food products and processing procedures to ensure safety for consumption and compliance with health regulations.
Write and revise safety regulations and codes, and provide expert testimony and guidance on health-related problems.
Conduct air quality, noise, temperature, or radiation testing and evaluate potential health hazards linked to product misuse.
Evaluate safety of product designs and conduct research on industry standards for product safety.
Develop training programs in safety laws, regulations, and hazardous condition monitoring for employees.
Compile, analyze, and interpret statistical data related to occupational illnesses and accidents to improve workplace safety standards.
Conduct research into the feasibility, design, operation, or performance of robotic mechanisms or systems.
Investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures.
Design software to control robotic systems for applications in manufacturing or other operations.
Monitor nuclear facility operations to identify practices that violate safety regulations or jeopardize safe operations.
Develop or validate product-specific test protocols or acceptance thresholds for quality control testing or performance measurement.
Create or maintain formal engineering documents, such as schematics or quality control protocols for operations.
Evaluate the safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of biomedical equipment and assist in their acquisition and use.
Design fire detection equipment and recommend fire prevention measures to ensure compliance with safety standards.
Conduct research related to a range of nanotechnology topics, including nanomaterials and nanodevices, to drive innovation.
Initiate corrective actions or order shutdowns in nuclear facilities during emergency situations.
Attend workshops, seminars, or conferences to obtain information regarding fire prevention and protection.
Study the relationships between ignition sources and materials to determine how fires start and develop plans for the prevention of destruction by fire, wind, and water.
Consult with authorities to discuss safety regulations and recommend changes as necessary, and confer with owners, violators, or authorities to explain regulations or recommend remedial actions.
Develop or review fire exit plans and conduct fire exit drills to monitor and evaluate evacuation procedures.
Inspect and monitor construction sites to ensure adherence to safety standards, building codes, or specifications, and identify corrective actions to bring properties into compliance with fire codes.
Prepare reports, deliver presentations, and document project activities and data related to safety inspections, fire code violations, and corrective recommendations.
Write detailed reports of fire inspections performed and recommend corrective actions for fire code violations observed.
Teach public education programs on fire safety and prevention, and educate customers on energy efficiency or topics related to safety in construction.
Examine commercial sites to determine health or safety issues related to planned projects and inspect facilities or installations to determine their environmental impact.
Conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify or quantify problems, such as air quality, asbestos, or other environmental hazards.
Inspect properties that store, handle, and use hazardous materials to ensure compliance with laws, codes, and regulations.
Recommend changes to fire prevention, inspection, and fire code endorsement procedures and study fire safety codes for permit issuance.
Conduct fire code compliance follow-ups to ensure corrective actions have been taken for any violations.
Review blueprints and plans for new or remodeled buildings to ensure they meet fire safety codes and compliance standards.
Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, records of convicted arsonists, and permitting applications for construction or remediation projects.
Inspect and test fire protection systems to ensure compliance with applicable laws, codes, and standards.
Analyze evidence and other information to determine the probable cause of fires or explosions, including investigating suspected arson cases.
Supervise staff, planning their work and evaluating performance, while managing or coordinating fitness and wellness programs and services.
Participate in continuing education classes to maintain current knowledge of industry.
Direct facility maintenance or repair and ensure safe and clean facilities and equipment.
Verify staff credentials, schedule staff, and inform them of job responsibilities or performance expectations.
Recruit, interview, hire employees, and conduct training sessions for wellness and fitness staff.
Respond to customer inquiries or complaints and track attendance or participation related to wellness events.
Coordinate facility schedules to maximize usage and efficiency, including planning programs of events or activity schedules.
Develop and maintain fitness and wellness programs or classes, ensuring a diversity of offerings.
Monitor operations to ensure compliance with health, safety, or hygiene standards in fitness and wellness services.
Develop marketing strategies and campaigns to promote wellness and fitness programs or a healthy lifestyle.
Maintain client databases, wellness-related schedules, and documentation of compliance activities.
Assess employee performance, suggest improvement ways, and train staff in company procedures or regulatory policies.
Administer first aid in emergency situations.
Coordinate or conduct internal investigations of issues such as employee theft or corporate policy violations.
Develop and maintain partnerships with law enforcement and retail loss prevention communities.
Inspect equipment, materials, and facilities to ensure they are safe, free of defects, and meet specifications.
Maintain or verify records of time, materials, and expenditures or crew activities.
Dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to emergencies reported via telephone or radio.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and effective use of materials and equipment.
Enforce safety rules, regulations, and provide training to ensure compliance.
Prepare and submit reports on work activities, operations, and incidents, including accidents.
Resolve worker issues collaboratively and recommend personnel actions, including evaluations and disciplinary actions.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet operational goals.
Confer with customers, supervisors, and contractors to exchange information and resolve issues.
Train staff in operational procedures and assign training tasks appropriately.
Requisition necessary personnel, supplies, and services for operations.
Review orders, production schedules, and relevant notices to determine work sequences.
